questions.question: On an 802.1Q trunk, what happens to a frame tagged with a VLAN that is not in the allowed list on the receiving switch?
options: It is retagged with the native VLAN
questions.explanation: The allowed-VLAN list is a per-trunk filter; unlisted tags are dropped at ingress to the trunk on the receiving side.

questions.question: An access switch won STP root election. What is the likely operational impact?
options: Interswitch traffic detours through the access layer and core uplinks may block
All VLANs are merged into one
questions.explanation: With the root at the edge, path costs steer traffic through low-bandwidth access links and block redundant core-to-core uplinks.

questions.question: Which command hardens the intended root bridge on a Cisco core switch?
questions.explanation: root primary lowers the priority so the core wins root election; BPDU Guard is for access ports, not the core.
